texto: I feel alone.I don't mean i feel lonely; I mean i feel alone, the same way i feel the blanket resting on my body, or the feathers of my pillow under my head, or the tight string of my sleep pants twisted up around my waist. I feel alone as if it were an actual thing, seeping throughout this whole level like mist blanketing a field, reaching into all the hidden corners of my room and finding nothing living but me. It's a cold sort of feeling, this.

texto: I guess when someone's gone from your life for a while, all you think about are the big things. The big regrets, the could-have, should-haves. Or the big moments, the memories that are going to be with you forever, those life-changing moments, like first kisses and first confessions and first trusts. And you think about the lasts too: the last kiss, the last words, the last moments.

texto: Don’t you understand? You are Elder. When you take my role as Eldest, you must dedicate your whole life to this one idea: you are the caretaker of every single person on the ship. You must always be everything to everyone on board.

texto: I try not to look obvious as I wait for Mom’s answer. texto: What else can you tell me?” Dad stares at me. “What have you learned while you were awake?”I learned that life is so, so fragile. I learned that you can know someone for just days and never forget the impression he left on you. I learned that art can be beautiful and sad at the same time. I learned that if someone loves you, he’ll wait for you to love him back. I learned that how much you want something doesn’t determine whether you get it or not, that “no” might not be enough, that life isn’t fair, that my parents can’t save me, that maybe no one can. “Nothing much,” I mutter.

texto: What you really want to know,” I say, “is how to make sure we all don’t just rip each other apart, right?” The fight earlier is way too fresh in our minds. We are a powder keg; just a spark will blow us apart.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Beth Revis que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “What you really want to know,” I say, “is how to make sure we all don’t just rip each other apart…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, across-the-universe, amy-martin, com foco em amy-martin e apart.

texto: And this is what she wants to do to people. Let them have their own lives, until she wants them. Give them the strength of giants, but not the power to control it.
explicacao: Citação de Beth Revis sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “And this is what she wants to do to people. Let them have their own lives, until she wants them. …” articula vida, control, manipulation em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como manipulation e power.
